steps: Launch the app on a machine whose hardware encoders you know.
Open Settings -> Export and click the Encoder Select.
Confirm the listed options match the expected set (Auto + CPU + the available HW encoders for this machine).
On a machine without an NVIDIA GPU, confirm NVENC is not listed.
Time app launch on a machine where ffmpeg is on PATH; confirm time-to-window is not delayed by more than 1 s vs. baseline.
Temporarily move the ffmpeg binary out of PATH and relaunch.
Confirm the app still launches and the Encoder Select shows only Auto + CPU.
On a machine with NVENC available, set Settings -> Export -> Encoder to NVENC.
Confirm codec_name is h264 and a stream tag (encoder/handler_name) references nvenc.
Set Settings -> Export -> Encoder to CPU and export the same 10 s fixture.
Run ffprobe and confirm the encoder tag references libx264.
On a clean VM with no GPU drivers, force Settings -> Export -> Encoder = NVENC.
Confirm a non-blocking toast appears stating the hardware encoder failed and CPU was used.
Confirm the exported file plays and ffprobe shows libx264 as the encoder.

AC-004-a.command: Run cut-drift-fuzzer agent on eval/fixtures/toaster_example.mp4 with the chosen hardware encoder

steps: Confirm zero matches. The codec strings only appear in src-tauri/src/managers/export/encoders.rs.
